source.notes: Optimus _ Freight Buffalo - 2026_05_12 16_23 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Optimus/Freight Buffalo rep Max Riemer pitched a three-person freight brokerage agency (Mike Mcmahon of One Logistics Network) on the platform as a ZoomInfo alternative for contacts, phone numbers, and lane data. Max walked through the new per-user pricing ($175/user, ~$525/mo vs. the $750 enterprise tier), demoed six-source real-time contact enrichment and lane maps, and ran a live National Beef lookup at Mike's request. Mike was highly enthusiastic and effectively closed himself; agreed next steps are Max sending contracts and Mike emailing Jared for test-shipper data ahead of the June 1 onboarding cohort.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max was attentive and responsive rather than scripted, picking up on the buyer's cues (cradle-to-grave workflow, small-agency status) and answering each question directly; acoustic signal shows healthy pitch variation (8.69 st) and steady arousal, only mildly below his own baseline.

evidence.quote: I'm guessing it's it's cradle to grave, right?
that's what I was for two years prior to this. So, I know I know what it's like.
factoring companies for the most part.

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could summarize it: cheaper per-user data than the enterprise tier, more/better contacts than ZoomInfo from six real-time-enriched sources plus lane visibility, backed by a concrete customer proof point.

evidence.quote: a team of three at 175 a user is 525 a month. And it's the same platform, same UI, same data.
we believe that we provide more data than Zoom Info or have better contacts because we source from six different um guys. So, Seamless, WhizA
he moved 14 shipments with them the first like two weeks

relevance.explanation: The pitch was tailored to this specific buyer: he framed the per-user pricing around their three-person small-agency setup, connected on their cradle-to-grave workflow, and offered to test data against the buyer's own shippers, running a live lookup on the prospect Mike named.

evidence.quote: I just like to get this out of the blue with the with the smaller brokerages to sort of get
what we like to do is sort of on this call or another call run through prospects or shippers you know well to sort of test the data

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked strong qualifying questions up front (agency status, team tenure, workflow), but with a buyer who explicitly wanted to start immediately he let the prospect drive the close and routed the first action to a colleague (Jared) without locking a signing or follow-up date beyond the June 1 cohort.

evidence.quote: Three sales guys. Are y'all like an agency
email Jared at get Optimus AI and uh he'll uh he'll shoot you over some info on three three shippers if you have some hot leads.
our next onboarding cohort's going to be June 1st.

Max Riemer.coaching_action: When a buyer signals they want to move now, personally own each next step and lock a specific signing/follow-up date on the call instead of handing the immediate action off to a colleague.

signals.signal: Explicit in-his-own-words statement that the product fits his need
signals.quote: Max, I This is kind of like exactly what I'm looking for.

signals.signal: Expressed urgency to get started quickly
signals.quote: I'd like to kind of get this going as soon as we can.

signals.signal: Buyer-owned commitment to move forward and request for contracts
signals.quote: Send me um send me both contracts. I'm probably going to do the uh the cheaper one, you know, but let's get going.

signals.signal: Stated intent to sign and begin using immediately
signals.quote: as soon as I sign the paper, let me start using the test link just to like get some s*** going.
